mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment rise, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive z-scores of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS yields positive 60-day returns in > 60% of historical occurrences, invalidating the stagflation penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Subdued real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and contractionary economic momentum, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and payroll employment confirms synchronized supply-side strength, driving immediate positive risk sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% in out-of-sample testing.
